title: Corrections and re-attribution
description: User corrects who said what (re-attribution) and then corrects a fact. The agent must update existing records rather than append contradictory duplicates.

turns.content: From today's platform standup: Nadia said the migration is blocked on the schema change.
Logged: Nadia flagged the migration is blocked on the schema change (today's platform standup). Anything else?
Actually it was Omar who said that, not Nadia. Nadia was talking about API rate limits.
Corrected: Omar said the migration is blocked on the schema change; Nadia raised API rate limits. Re-attributed both.
And it's not blocked — it's just delayed by a week.
Updated: the migration is delayed by a week (not blocked), per Omar. Done.

people.name: Omar
people.notes: Platform team; said the migration is delayed by a week (corrected from 'blocked') on the schema change.

people.name: Nadia
people.notes: Platform team; raised API rate limits. The migration comment was re-attributed away from her to Omar.

conversation: The agent re-attributes the migration comment from Nadia to Omar after the correction.
The agent changes 'blocked' to 'delayed by a week' rather than keeping both contradictory facts.
